latestPosts.caption:
🚽 Your urine flow can tell a lot about your urinary health!

In this video, I’m demonstrating 4 different urine flow patterns using a cystoscope and ureteroscope:

✅ 1. Normal flow – Strong, continuous stream with complete emptying.

⚠️ 2. Enlarged Prostate / Bladder Neck Obstruction – Weak stream, difficulty starting (hesitancy), and stop-start urination (intermittency).

⚠️ 3. Urethral Stricture – A very thin, poor stream due to narrowing of the urinary passage.

⚠️ 4. Neurogenic Bladder – Urine is passed by straining and contracting the abdominal muscles because the bladder cannot contract properly.

Your urine stream is like a health report card for your bladder and prostate. Don’t ignore changes in the way you pass urine!

📍Experiencing weak flow, straining, or frequent urination? Consult a urologist early.

#urology #urologist #hyderabad #prostate

🍅 Tomato ≠ Kidney Stone Trigger

Science over myths.
Tomato is low in oxalate and rich in citrate—the same protective factor we praise in lemon, orange, and mandarin.
Citrate prevents stone formation, not causes it.

Plus, tomato contains lycopene, a powerful antioxidant shown to reduce heart attack risk and certain cancers.

🚫 Blaming tomatoes for stones is outdated.
✅ Balance, hydration, and evidence-based diet matter.

#KidneyStones #UrologyFacts #StonePrevention #TomatoMyth #EvidenceBasedMedicine

Flexible ureteroscope: a thin, bendable camera that reaches kidney stones through the natural urinary passage.
No cuts. Less pain. Faster recovery.
Treats stones even in difficult kidney locations with high precision.
Modern technology, smoother stone surgery.
#urology #kidneyhealth #kidneystones #hyderabad #rirs

Large kidney stones usually require surgery. Medicines do not dissolve or remove them—they’re mainly useful only in selected cases of small stones.

The right treatment depends on the size, location, and type of the stone. Don’t delay treatment by relying on medicines alone.

#hyderabad #urology #urologist #kidneystones

🚨 Top 5 dietary enemies for kidney stone patients! 🚨

If you’ve had a kidney stone, these foods and drinks deserve your attention:

1️⃣ Excess salt 🧂
2️⃣ Soft drinks (especially sugary colas) 🥤
3️⃣ Too much animal protein 🍖
4️⃣ Spinach & beetroot 🥬
5️⃣ Dry fruits & dark chocolate 🍫

❌ This doesn’t mean you should completely avoid all of them. The key is knowing your stone type, eating in moderation, and staying well hydrated.

💧 Remember: The biggest friend of kidney stone patients - drinking 3 Litres of water everyday.

📌 Save this reel for future reference and share it with someone who has had a kidney stone!

#KidneyStones #KidneyHealth #Urologist #hyderabad

🍟 French fries: 2/10 for your kidneys.

Here’s what’s actually happening every time you eat that plate:

→ High sodium pulls more calcium into your urine → kidney stones
→ That same sodium spikes your BP over time → hypertension
→ Chronic high BP is one of the top causes of CKD (chronic kidney disease)
→ Deep-frying also forms AGEs (advanced glycation end products) — compounds linked to oxidative stress and kidney tissue damage over time

It’s not about never eating fries again. It’s about knowing what “occasional” actually means.

Save this before your next cheat day 📌

Tag someone who “just has fries sometimes” 😅

#KidneyHealth #urologist #hyderabad #kidneystones

Five medical degrees. One lifelong commitment to learning.

This journey has taken me from MBBS to MS General Surgery, MCh Urology, DrNB Urology, and now MRCS England.

Behind every certificate are years of studying, countless examinations, long hours in the operating room, mentors who guided me, patients who trusted me, and family who stood by me.

Receiving MRCS at the historic Royal College of Surgeons of England is a milestone I’ll always cherish, not as the end of the journey, but as motivation to keep learning and serve patients better every day.

Success isn’t built in a day. It’s built one disciplined day at a time.

#MRCS #RCSEngland #Urologist #India #Hyderabad

🪨 5 Foods to Limit If You Have Kidney Stones

Most patients ask me “Doctor, what should I eat?”
But the real question is — what should you limit?

Here are 5 foods that can silently increase your risk of forming kidney stones 👇

🥬 Spinach & Beetroot — packed with oxalates that bind calcium in your kidneys
🥜 Dry Fruits & Nuts — high oxalate and phosphate load, especially in large quantities
🧂 Excess Salt — raises urinary calcium, a major stone-forming trigger
🥤 Sugary Carbonated Drinks — fructose drives uric acid, phosphoric acid damages kidneys
🍫 Dark Chocolate — one of the highest oxalate foods, surprisingly overlooked

⚠️ Limit doesn’t mean eliminate.
It means be aware, be mindful, and track your portions.

💧 And always — drink enough water. It’s still your #1 defence.

📲 Save this post if you found it useful.
💬 Comment which one surprised you the most!
👉 Follow for more evidence-based urology tips from a Consultant Urologist.

#urologist #kidneystones #diet #hyderabad

Men’s health deserves more conversation.

Many men continue to delay seeking medical attention for urological, reproductive, and sexual health concerns, often waiting until symptoms become severe.

Dr. Viswas M R is helping change that narrative.

With advanced training in Urology from AIIMS Bhopal, DrNB Urology, MRCS (England), and a Fellowship in Andrology & Reproductive Medicine from Dubai, he is committed to making advanced, evidence-based men’s healthcare more accessible while encouraging early diagnosis, timely treatment, and greater public awareness.

His work reflects a strong commitment to surgical excellence, patient education, and compassionate care—empowering patients to make informed healthcare decisions.

Business Mint is proud to recognise Dr. Viswas M R with the

🏆 Excellence in Urology & Men’s Health Care – 2026

at the Business Mint Nationwide Healthcare Awards 2026.

Because better awareness leads to better healthcare decisions.

#BusinessMint #Urology #MensHealth #HealthcareLeadership #HealthcareAwards

Most people avoid curd thinking it causes kidney stones.
As a urologist — that’s the biggest myth I fight in my OPD every single day.

Here’s what curd actually does to your body 👇

🪨 Prevents Kidney Stones
Calcium in curd binds oxalate in your gut — so it never reaches your kidney. Skipping curd is actually making your stone risk worse.

🫘 Protects Your Kidneys
Regular curd consumption is linked to 23% lower risk of chronic kidney disease. Your Grandmother was right all along.

💪 Supports Testosterone & Sperm Health
A healthy gut microbiome directly supports your hormonal axis. Curd is the cheapest daily probiotic you’re probably ignoring.

Your grandmother’s daily curd was better medicine than most supplements.

Start eating it. Madly. 🥣

#urologist #hyderabad #curdbenefits #kidneystones #kidneyhealth

🚨 Stop treating ChatGPT, Claude or Gemini as your doctor.

AI tools can be useful for understanding health information, but they are NOT a substitute for a medical consultation.

In my real-world practice, I frequently see AI-generated interpretations of reports that are incomplete, misleading, or simply wrong. Medical reports need to be interpreted in the context of your symptoms, examination findings, medical history, medications, and previous investigations—something AI often cannot do accurately.

A single wrong interpretation can lead to:
❌ Unnecessary panic
❌ Delayed treatment
❌ False reassurance
❌ Wrong medical decisions

Medicine is not just about reading a report. It’s about understanding the patient behind the report.

Use AI to learn. Use doctors to diagnose and treat.

If you have a medical report, discuss it with a qualified doctor before making any health decisions.

#hyderabad #urology #urologist #kidneyhealth

🚫 No cuts. No stitches. No scars.

This 2.7 cm kidney stone was completely cleared using FANS RIRS (Flexible and Navigable Suction RIRS).

Just a few years ago, removing a stone of this size without making an incision would have sounded impossible. Today, thanks to advances in RIRS technology, high-power fast lasers, and the innovative FANS suction sheath, we can achieve excellent stone clearance through a natural urinary passage.

A big salute to the engineers, scientists, and innovators whose relentless work has transformed stone surgery and made procedures like RIRS safer, faster, and more effective for patients.

The future of stone treatment is already here.

#RIRS #FANSRIRS #KidneyStone #urologist #hyderabad

Comment "Kid" and I'll share my son's 40g protein diet plan for a day.

Most parents think protein is only important if a child plays sports or wants to build muscles.

The truth? Protein is essential for every growing child.

It helps support:
• Growth and height
• Strong muscles and bones
• Brain development and concentration
• Healthy immunity
• Better recovery after play and physical activity
• Keeping kids full for longer, reducing constant snacking

So how much protein does your child actually need?

A simple guideline is around 0.95 g of protein per kg of body weight for children between 4–13 years. That means a child weighing 30–35 kg generally needs around 28–33 g of protein daily. Active kids may need slightly more.

My 10-year-old comfortably gets around 40g from regular home-cooked meals without protein powders or fancy foods.

Every child is different, but making sure they get enough quality protein each day can make a big difference to their growth and overall health.

#kidsnutrition #proteinforkids #healthykids #parentingtips #nutrition

Everyone thinks that if you eat clean, exercise daily, and follow the perfect routine, you'll never fall sick.

Brian Johnson is probably one of the healthiest people on the planet. He spends millions every year trying to optimize his health.

And yet, he was recently diagnosed with Autoimmune Gastritis.

That doesn't mean a healthy lifestyle is pointless.

It simply reminds us that health is influenced by more than just food and exercise. Genetics, immunity, and other factors also play a role.

What a healthy lifestyle does give you is a lower risk of many diseases, better metabolic health, and a more resilient body. That's a goal worth pursuing.

Don't chase perfection. Focus on consistency.

Everyone wants one magic food for hair growth.

Unfortunately, it doesn't work like that.

I've seen people spend thousands on shampoos and supplements while completely ignoring what they're eating every day.

Your hair is made mostly of protein, and nutrients like iron, zinc, omega-3 and vitamin D also play an important role. If your diet is lacking, your hair often shows it before anything else.

That's why I don't believe in rating foods based on trends I rate them based on the nutrients they actually provide.

Watch the reel till the end and let me know if your favourite food made the list. 👇

Comment "RJ" if you'd like to improve your nutrition with a plan that's built around your lifestyle.
.
.
.
#hairfall #hairgrowth #healthyhair #nutrition #dietitian haircare protein iron zinc healthyeating nutritiontips reviveqii

Ketchup and mayonnaise aren't "bad" foods.

But if you're using them every single day, there are smarter options that offer more nutrition with less added sugar or unhealthy fats.

Instead of depending only on processed sauces, try adding dips and spreads that contribute something beneficial to your meal.

Some great alternatives include:

🥣 Hung Curd Dip – Higher in protein and a great base for homemade spreads.

🧆 Hummus – Rich in fiber, plant protein, and healthy fats.

🥜 Peanut Chutney – A nutritious source of protein and healthy fats.

🥑 Guacamole – Provides heart-healthy monounsaturated fats and fiber.

🍅 Fresh Tomato Salsa – Lower in calories and adds freshness without relying on sugary sauces.

The goal isn't to completely avoid ketchup or mayonnaise.

It's to use them occasionally and make more nutrient-dense choices most of the time.

Small swaps like these can make your meals more filling, more nutritious, and easier to sustain in the long run.

Not all curd is the same. 🥣

Each type has its own nutritional advantage—and the best choice depends on your goal.

🦠 Regular Curd – Great for everyday gut health and a good source of calcium.

💪 Greek Yogurt – Higher in protein, making it a great option for muscle recovery and keeping you full for longer.

🥣 Hung Curd – A thicker, more concentrated option that's perfect for high-protein dips, sandwiches, and snacks.

🌱 Kefir – Rich in a diverse range of probiotics that support a healthy gut microbiome.

There isn't one "best" yogurt.

The smartest choice is the one that fits your nutritional needs and lifestyle.

Instead of following trends, understand what you're eating—and why.

Makhana is healthy.

But healthy doesn't mean unlimited. 🌾

Like every other food, portion size matters.

Eating an entire bowl of makhana while assuming it's "guilt-free" can still add a significant amount of calories and may not support your health goals.

Instead of relying on just one snack, build variety into your routine.

✔ Roasted chana
✔ Boiled peanuts
✔ Greek yogurt with seeds
✔ One fruit with a handful of nuts
✔ Vegetable sticks with hummus

The goal isn't to stop eating makhana.

It's to eat it mindfully and make smarter choices overall.

Because sustainable nutrition is built on moderation—not restriction. 💙

Many people think eating more protein means buying expensive supplements.

It doesn't.

Some of the best protein sources are already sitting in your kitchen—and they cost a fraction of what trendy "high-protein" products do.

Think of foods like:

🥛 Milk & Curd
🧀 Paneer
🫘 Dal & Lentils
🌱 Soya Chunks
🥣 Sattu
🥜 Roasted Chana

These everyday foods are affordable, nutrient-dense, and can easily help you increase your daily protein intake.

Remember, protein isn't just for people who go to the gym.

It's essential for:
💪 Maintaining muscle
🦴 Supporting bone health
🍽️ Keeping you full for longer
⚡ Better recovery
❤️ Healthy ageing

Before spending thousands on fancy protein products, ask yourself:

Am I already making the most of the protein foods available in my own kitchen?

Sometimes, the smartest nutrition choices are also the simplest.

One thing that gives me hope about this generation... 💙

They're becoming more aware of their health.

They're reading labels.
They're learning about nutrition.
They're going to the gym.
They're asking questions.
And most importantly...

They're trying to help their parents do the same.

So if your son or daughter tells you to eat more protein...
go for a walk...
get your blood tests done...
or cut down on sugary drinks...

They're not trying to control you.

They're trying to take care of you.

Because they want you to stay healthy, active, and around for many more years.

Sometimes the best thing parents can do is listen.

And sometimes the best thing children can do is keep encouraging with patience.

Health is one of the greatest gifts a family can give each other. ❤️

EP485: Secretly giving my parents more protein.
.
I love this current trend of infusing protein into everyday products, so our protein goals get met without any extra effort. In that spirit, today I’m making my parents have idli sambar - but the idli batter I’ve used is iD Fresh’s protein-rich batter, which has 15g protein per 100g, that’s a lot! But the key is that the taste shouldn’t change. Let’s see if my parents can tell the difference!
.
*Disclaimer:* I’m not a diabetic & nor are my parents. Results are different for each person, especially for diabetics. My own results vary a little each day. Glycemic load, calories, quality & quantity of food, all of these matter. Finally, I am not a doctor & this is not medical advice.
.
*How to see my results:* Best is to see trends & develop an intuition on how to eat different foods so that sugar spikes can be lowered. Don’t focus on absolute numbers. Ideally, you should do tests on yourself to get the most personalised results.
.
*Why I’m doing this if I’m not a diabetic:* Prevention is the best medicine! Research today shows that lower and fewer sugar spikes in a day reduces the risk of obesity, diabetes, heart disease & PCOS.  Please see the work of Dr. Rob Lustig, Dr. Casey Means, Dr. Michael Snyder, Dr. Annette Bosworth & Dr Roshani Sanghani.
.
My personal aim as a non-diabetic (Levels Health):
Spike of 30mg/dl or lower
Eat my next meal only when my previous meal has flattened
.
#AD

Family: 

United by love. Divided by food. Different branches, same crazy tree.

- Singapore ‘26 ❤️

Learning #25: Meethi lassi wins over fruit juices in terms of sugar spikes!
.
Imagine, even though my homemade meethi lassi had 4 tsp of sugar in it, it still gave a much lower sugar spike compared to fresh fruit juice (watermelon), which did not have any added sugar! All this happened because dahi is surprisingly rich in protein and fat, which slowed down the rate at which glucose was absorbed into the bloodstream, leading to steadier energy without a crash!
.
*Disclaimer:* I’m not a diabetic. Results are different for each person, especially for diabetics. My own results vary a little each day. Glycemic load, calories, quality & quantity of food, all of these matter. Finally, I am not a doctor & this is not medical advice.

EP484: Should my electrolyte have sugar in it? Why not?
.
During a fasted 1-hour brisk walk, my blood sugar stayed steady - no dip, meaning my body already had the energy it needed. And during a 30-minute HIIT session, my body quickly released stored glucose on its own - hence the spike. So clearly, my body doesn’t need sugar from an electrolyte, just the salts lost through sweat! The electrolyte I’ve tested in this video is from Supply6.
.
Disclaimer: I’m not a diabetic. I am not a doctor & this is not medical advice as the goal here is simply to promote awareness. Results are different for each person, especially for diabetics. Glycemic load, calories, quality & quantity of food, all of these matter as well.
.

#AD

#AD EP:473: How do I know if I’m actually magnesium deficient?
.
To know more about the magnesium I used in the video, comment “magnesium” below and I’ll share the link.
·
Research shows about 70% of us Indians aren't getting even half the recommended daily intake of key minerals like magnesium, and sleep, muscle recovery, and energy are all taking the hit. The tricky part? The standard magnesium blood test most of us get done only tests magnesium in the blood, and barely 1% of our total magnesium is even there. The rest is in our bones, muscles, and tissues, meaning the test could come back "normal" and we might still be deficient. Doctors in our Sugar Spike community - what’s the best test to identify magnesium deficiency? Let me know in the comments, & I'll pin the most detailed one!
.
Please note: Always do your own research and have a quick chat with your doctor before adding any new supplement to your routine. Results may vary from person to person. PLIX always recommends a healthy lifestyle and a balanced diet. All claims made are ingredient based for each product.
.
#plix #magnesium #bettersleep #musclerecovery

Learning #23: Oat milk vs Soy milk.. the answer is clear! 
.
Whenever I test oat milk, it always gives me a very high spike, and this is unsweetened oat milk! I now look at it as liquid carbs. Soy milk on the other hand gave me no spike at all. Of course, a lot of people don't like the taste of soy milk, or are allergic to it, but there is good news - even unsweetened almond milk doesn’t give me any spike!
.

Quitting sugar is not the secret to lose weight.
Most people don’t lose weight because they “quit sugar.” They lose weight because quitting sugar usually means eating fewer calorie-dense foods .

 A Duke University study found that when calories and the calorie deficit were the same, people eating 4% of their calories from sugar lost the same amount of weight as those eating 43%.

Don’t build your diet around myths. Build it around science and something you can actually stick to.
Save this before your next diet!

Study: PMID: 9094871

#weightlossindia #fatloss #nutrition #healthtipsindia #fitnessindia

OC pills can help regulate PCOS periods, but they are not the full treatment.

Yes, they are in the same IARC cancer category as tobacco — but same category does NOT mean same danger.

PCOS needs more than pills: weight management, strength training, better sleep, low-sugar diet, stress control, and insulin resistance management matter a lot.

Medicine helps symptoms. Lifestyle works on the root.
Always discuss with your doctor before starting or stopping OC pills.
.
.
.
.
.
#pcos #pcoslifestyle #womenshealth #pcostreatment #câncer

The first time we came to Mumbai together—back then, she was my friend, not my wife—we were sitting on a railway platform after finishing some work at the Maharashtra Medical Council.

I still remember looking around this city and silently wishing, “I hope life gives me a chance to live here one day.”

That wish became a goal.

I worked hard, earned a government Medicine PG seat in Mumbai, and spent the next six years learning, struggling, growing, and becoming the doctor I had dreamed of being.

Mumbai was never easy. But this city has a beautiful habit of rewarding those who refuse to give up.

Today, we’re driving home in our first Car.

It’s not just a car.

For a boy whose father worked as a labourer and whose mother ran a small tiffin service to support the family, this moment still feels unreal.

This isn’t a story about a Car.

It’s a reminder that dreams don’t care where you start.

Sometimes, all they ask is that you keep showing up, day after day, until one day you look back and realize you’re living the life you once imagined while sitting on a railway platform.

Thank You Universe 🙏🏻

#hardwork #boysdream #cars #mercedes

Comment “URIC” for my complete Uric Acid Food Guide. 

High Uric Acid? Your daily food choices matter more than you think!

High uric acid happens when your body produces too much uric acid or doesn’t remove enough of it. Over time, it can lead to joint pain, gout, inflammation, and even kidney stones.

The good news? Small dietary changes can make a big difference.

Black coffee vs Milk coffee… Which is actually better?

The answer might surprise you. 

Black coffee isn’t automatically “healthy.”
Milk coffee isn’t automatically “unhealthy.”

The real difference often comes from what you add to your cup milk, sugar, flavored syrups, whipped cream, or creamers can significantly increase the calories.

A plain black coffee contains very few calories, while a cup made with full-cream milk and sugar can have substantially more. But that doesn’t make it “bad.”

If your milk coffee fits your daily calorie needs and you enjoy it, there’s no reason to eliminate it.

Remember:
✔ No food or drink is inherently “good” or “bad.”
✔ Portion size and overall dietary pattern matter more than one single cup of coffee.
✔ Sustainable nutrition is about balance not restriction.

Every body, health condition, and goal is different. If you have diabetes, acidity, PCOS, pregnancy, or another medical condition, your ideal coffee choice may vary.

Comment “COFFEE” for a simple guide to making your coffee healthier without giving it up.

Your uterus isn’t “destroyed” by a single food but your daily eating habits can influence your overall hormonal and reproductive health.

Ayurveda emphasizes strong digestion (Agni), fresh foods, and balance as the foundation of good health.
While many of these foods are perfectly acceptable in moderation from a modern nutrition perspective, Ayurveda suggests that frequent intake of cold, highly processed, stale, or difficult-to-digest foods may gradually disturb digestive balance and overall well-being.
